# Helen Leung

> Life science investor and nanoscientist working at the intersection of drug development, venture capital, and health equity.

## Who I am

Helen Leung is a scientist and life science investor with a background spanning academic research, pharmaceutical industry drug development, and venture capital. Her work sits at the convergence of nanoscience, translational medicine, and cross-border investment — with particular expertise in Europe–China life science activity.

PLACEHOLDER: Add 2–3 sentences in your own voice describing your current primary focus and what makes your perspective distinctive.

## Current Work

PLACEHOLDER: Describe your current role(s). Are you actively investing, advising, researching, or a combination? Name any firm or fund affiliation if applicable.

### Nanoscience in Drug Development
Helen's core scientific expertise is in the application of nanoscale materials and structures to drug delivery and therapeutic design. PLACEHOLDER: Add a sentence on your specific research contribution or approach.

### Europe–China VC/PE Activity Tracker
An ongoing project tracking venture capital and private equity flows between Europe and China across the life sciences sector, covering biopharma, MedTech, diagnostics, and digital health. PLACEHOLDER: Add methodology, output format, and intended audience.

### Health Equity
PLACEHOLDER: Describe your health equity work — the communities, the initiatives, your role.

## What I work on

- Nanoscience applications in drug development and delivery
- Translational medicine and scientific due diligence
- Venture capital and private equity in life sciences, with a focus on Europe–China cross-border activity
- Health equity in clinical research and healthcare access
- AI-driven materials discovery (Altrove)
